entities.name: 影响力 (influence)
entities.how_described: 本集的核心主题，被定义为增加好想法存活下来的几率，而不是为自己的利益搞政治操纵；其本质是把高管当作核心用户，用同理心去理解他们，以增加获得支持的可能。

evidence.quote: And she has gotten very, very uniquely good at the art and science of influence, and in particular, influencing executives.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Influence might be the single highest leverage skill for product leaders outside of AI.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We actually get into how AI is changing the skill of influence.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I've never heard a podcast conversation get deep into the art and science of influence, how to actually change people's minds, and the mistakes that people make when they're trying to influence leaders.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We're going to be talking about the skill and the art of influence, and in particular, influencing executives and leaders.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Influence and building momentum behind great ideas is the way that great products actually get built.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And if you don't have that influence, if you don't have the buy-in and the backing of your key stakeholders, of your executives, you can't build great products.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I always tell them, "That's just you're not able to influence them and convince them that you're right."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I want to dive deeper into this stuff, but first, something on my mind is some people might be turned off by this topic in terms of, it may feel icky of just like, "Oh, I don't want to try to be this manipulative person, and deal with politics and influence.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Influence is about increasing the odds that your good ideas survive.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think what happens is people actually let ego and let their own narrative, their own scope of influence, get in the way of empathy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then a second lever is trying to not go into a conversation, even though you are trying to influence them.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And it also sounds like a really good way to influence is you take the reins and here's where I think we could go with this, and here's the answer to your question.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: You need to influence people in power to agree with your approach.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Before we get into AI's impact and all this stuff, are there any other common mistakes people make when they are trying to get better at the skill of influence or just any other really powerful tactics for increasing your ability to influence leaders?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: How is AI changing the skill of influence?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so it's actually an incredibly exciting place, but the act of influence, the act of stakeholder management, the act of learning...
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: ... active influence.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You are still working with millions of users, and alongside distribution is trust, and that is a function of influence and empathy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Now that agents are a part of the workforce and increasingly, is there anything there around influence that is valuable for people to start thinking about to learn how to do?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I think agents are absolutely something we have to think about with relation to influence, because we are basically all directors of work now, right?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: What's so funny about this, essentially we're helping the agent learn the skills we've been talking about to influence you.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I think that something that has taken me a while in my career is how to be authentic to myself in influence, in relationships, in trust building.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think all types of personalities can, in their own authentic way, communicate with the people around them to build trust, to build influence in the way that feels true to them.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 智能体 (agents)
entities.how_described: 本集结尾讨论了智能体作为加速工作的“同事”，指出现在每个人都像是工作的主管，需要用这套影响他人的方法来训练智能体同事，给它们提供目标背景并设定护栏。

evidence.quote: Fire off a hundred agents and we'll go build it and launch it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Maybe one more question along these lines, just agents now running as colleagues.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Now that agents are a part of the workforce and increasingly, is there anything there around influence that is valuable for people to start thinking about to learn how to do?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We talk about this a lot, especially when we think about identity, security, compliance, that agents are a teammate for accelerating work, and the risks that that also provides because of the places where agents fall down.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think agents are absolutely something we have to think about with relation to influence, because we are basically all directors of work now, right?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: If we have an army of agents or this hundred new colleagues that joined our team, how would we onboard new junior team members who don't already understand our product philosophy, what's important to us, and how do we codify that for ourselves?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You should just feed this podcast to a bunch of agents and see if they can do this better than me, see what their advice is.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: No, but I really think at this point we are in a place where it is a back and forth and where we should be using agents and AI as a really smart colleague that never gets irritated by our questions.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We'll add that to the..." All the ideas you've shared, agents are so good at.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so I think one of the ways that we actually have to use AI and agents is to help our brains be most effective, is to clear out distraction, to point out the most important things, to... And I think this is where people really need to make some tough decisions.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 护栏 (guardrails)
entities.how_described: 本集在谈论使用智能体时提到，指出由于智能体会犯错或产生幻觉，我们必须明确设定护栏，界定在哪里需要人类介入、进行判断和把关。

evidence.quote: But I also think the flip side of this is guardrails.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 上下文切换 (context switching)

entities.name: 甘特图 (Gantt chart)
entities.how_described: 本集用来比喻过去产品经理常引以为傲的执行统筹技能；嘉宾指出，当 AI 比人更擅长做数据分析和会议记录时，甘特图不再是 PM 的核心杠杆。

evidence.quote: So I think that product managers for a long time have made their careers on being the most type A, the Gantt chart master, the best note taker in the business.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 金字塔原理 (Pyramid)
entities.how_described: 本集提到的一种向高管汇报的方法，即反向呈现信息：先说结论和建议，再讲探索过程，最后放证据。

evidence.quote: Have you ever looked into the Minto Pyramid way of presenting stuff?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 产品市场契合度 (product market fit)
entities.how_described: 本集提到由于执行难度下降，产品经理需要利用工具快速构建 V1 版本，从而更快地获得关于产品市场契合度和用户价值的信号。

evidence.quote: My podcast guest and I love talking about craft and taste and agency and product market fit.

evidence.quote: And I actually think it's just so incredibly exciting because PMs and other functions, engineers, designers are so empowered to bring that first version to bear to get that momentum, to get that signal on product market fit, on user benefit, and then build momentum from that point in a much faster cycle.
